jobs.prompt: Create a clean academic paper figure comparing RehearFitEncoder and RehearFitLateShrinkEncoder as two encoder backbones. Use one shared input spectrogram on the left, then split into two compact horizontal architecture lanes. Top lane: RehearFitEncoder with six CNN blocks labeled channels 1->4->8->16->32->64->128 and flatten 6912. Bottom lane: RehearFitLateShrinkEncoder with the first four blocks identical 1->4->8->16->32, then late shrink 32->16->8 and flatten 432. Visually highlight that only the late high-channel part changes. Add small metric badges: parameters 609,090 vs 88,074, forward speed 2.894 vs 2.812 ms/batch. Keep labels sparse and accurate. White background, vector schematic, teal blue orange accents, conference-paper style.

Create a clean conference-paper concept figure explaining why RehearFitLateShrinkEncoder reduces FC pressure. Show one shared CNN front end with Conv1-Conv4 identical, then a zoomed comparison of the last two convolution layers. Standard RehearFitEncoder expands to 64 then 128 channels, producing a large 6912-d flatten feature and a heavy FC head. LateShrink reduces to 16 then 8 channels, producing a compact 432-d flatten feature and lighter FC head. Include visual width differences for feature tensors. Add concise numeric callouts: 609,090 params -> 88,074 params, about 85.5% fewer; speed 2.894 -> 2.812 ms/batch. Do not clutter with training metrics. White background, crisp vector blocks, sparse labels.

Create a clean academic comparison figure for a methods/results section. Use a single pipeline input spectrogram -> encoder -> FC regression head -> LTASS output, then place two encoder cards in the center: RehearFitEncoder and RehearFitLateShrinkEncoder. Each card shows channel pattern, flatten dimension, parameter count, and speed. RehearFitEncoder: 1->4->8->16->32->64->128, flatten 6912, 609,090 parameters, 2.894 ms/batch. RehearFitLateShrinkEncoder: 1->4->8->16->32->16->8, flatten 432, 88,074 parameters, 2.812 ms/batch. Highlight the tradeoff message: same early feature extractor, smaller late channels, much fewer parameters, no speed penalty. Use minimal text, paper-style badges, white background, teal blue orange accents.
